feed.title: SAP Community - Machine Learning
feed.subtitle: Machine Learning Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Example regarding classification and clustering Type Machine learning in SAP PaPM? Thanks in advance
entry.content:
Hi all,

Can someone please help me with an example regarding classification and clustering Type Machine learning in SAP PaPM?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How can I use pdf invoice for retraining the DOX Service using API call in SAP IRPA
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have a scenario where we are using Document Information Extraction APIs to extract data(values, confidence and coordinates) from Invoice. Now we want to use DOX API retraining with same invoice with correct information using SAP IRPA. How can we retrain the DOX using API calls in SAP IRPA with correct Invoice which is having Accurate Data?

Please provide any blogs or any other document with proper information through which we can retrain the Document Information Extraction Service for better results in next execution. any update on this will be helpful.

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How does artificial intelligence function?
entry.content: Artificial intelligence (AI) is the study of how robots replicate or mimic human intelligence, so clarify briefly how it functions and enable by several technologies.

entry.title: No Scriptserver available - hana on cloud while running python program with hana_ml library
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing below issue while trying to run a NaiveBayes ML model of hana_ml library from python program.

Issue : No ScriptServer available.

I am using Trial version of SAP BTP and HANA on Cloud.

Below is my Input Table.

Below is ML Model .

...

...

In above screenshot, it says "No ScriptServer available".

Please help me how to add/enable ScriptServer for hana on cloud.

entry.title: How to directly call the API of S4 hana database in BTP Process automation?
entry.content:
How can BTP Build Process Automation directly call the API of S4 PERSON_NAME database and how should it be implemented? Thank you very much.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Issue with consuming ML Scenario in SAP DI
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to perform complete ML scenario based on SAP Academy "Financial Fraud Detection with SAP DI Cloud" (RandomForestClassifier from sklearn package is used there). In that Academy last step is model training, and I also want to test this model with new data. So, for next steps I have used SAP blog about creating ML-scenario in SAP DI.

Whenever I send request from Postman I get response ""Error message": "An error occurred: y should be a 1d array, got an array of shape () instead." It doesn't matter if I send values from Postman or use constant values in the script, the error remains the same. If I try to predict result with the same constant values in Jupiter notebook in SAP DI everything is OK.

What do I need to do to make it work?

It is the only change in script comparing with the blog mentioned before:

list_data = [21, 1, 3534, 1853, 5387, 18,  1184, -2350]

i_data = np.asarray(list_data)
input_data = np.reshape(i_data, (1, -1))

y_pred = model.predict(input_data)

entry.content:
Hello All,

I have been given an Incident data set to provide insights out of the data. Also provide forecasts and predictions.

Please provide your suggestions to proceed on this do we have any prebuild service available.

Thanks,

entry.title: Machine Learning Extension in SAP HANA XSA (SOLMAN)
entry.content:
We have checked below blog and it is mentioned about machine learning extension deployment in SAP HAAN XSA option to find anamoly detection of alerts.

https://blogs.sap.com/2021/01/22/machine-learning-services-for-hybrid-operations-implementation-for-sap-solution-manager-7.2-and-sap-cloud-platform-sap-hana-xsa-part-2/

entry.title: S/4 HANA and Python Integration
entry.content: Is it allowed to create, and update tables in S/4 hana database using python scripts from outside S/4 ?

entry.title: Automatically Calculating tickets
entry.content:
Hi SAP communicaty,

I want to know if SAP is able to automatically calculate weights onto loading tickets.

for eg. i have a van with a 3.5 ton max wt limit. - the van will be weighed before it is loaded with products on. I want to use SAP to be able to automatically calculate the amount of product it can carry based on its current weighed amount - max permissable wt. limit.

if anyone could gimme a clue on if SAP can do this and how, it would be much appreciated.

entry.title: How to set privileges for HANA Machine Learning in Cloud Foundry ?
entry.content:
Hi,

We need to use HANA_ML python package in HANA database in a Cloud Foundry App in BTP. While the code works perfect when running it on a jupyter notebook, it gives unsufficient privilege errors when calling it from within a cloud foundry deployment. (e.g. the ataframe.create_dataframe_from_pandas method fails).

We use the same code and user/password combination in both cases.

Any help appreciated on how to set authorization or how to allow deletion of tables from the CF deployment.

Regards

Marcus

entry.title: Queries on SAP LLM and Joule?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are looking to enable Joule. However before doing so, we want to understand more about following question. Please share your thoughts on these questions.

1. Which LLM model is SAP using? Is it homegrown or Other models like GPT, BERT or LLAMA?

2. How is SAP addressing the hallucination issues?

3. Any other terms and condition that SAP want Client to accept in order to use Gen AI/Joule?
4. Is same LLM used in various modules or different LLM used ? For example for Job description one LLM, For SF chatboat another LLM model?
5. What are the update cadence for the SAP LLM Product like schedule release or Adhoc release?

6 .Where does this LLM resides and size of custom SAP LLM?

7. Once Joule is enabled, can we turn On/Off depending on modules?

entry.title: How to use HANA-ML python package in a HANA HDI Container and how to assign correct priveledges?
entry.content:
Hi ,

we are struggling with the use of an HDI container for machine learning with the python hana-ml package in Python.

In a previous question https://answers.sap.com/questions/13984833/how-to-set-privileges-for-hana-machine-learning-in.html?childToView=13984942 a topic on missing priviledges was raised. The answer in the end was to use the user/pw combination from the sharedaccesskey in the service definition. However, when using this user it needs to be hard coded. How can we assign priviledges "CREATE ANY" to the user which can be read via the python service object in python ? This is a different user. Also in the documentation it is not recommended to give CREATE ANY to a user in HDI container.

How can this issue be solved ?

Or is machine learning with python in general not allowed / recommended in a DHDI container ? That would however significantly reduce the value of the python approach.

Regards

Marcus

entry.title: What transport to use from SAP Learning QA to PROD?
entry.content:
Hello,

I hope you are fine,

Is it possible to enter all the elements in SAP Learning QA and then take them to Productive? Is there any transportation?

Thank you so much,

Kind regards

Thanks and Regards

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP HANA PAL Search table error: "Column doesn't exist"
entry.content:
Hello Community! 😄

When I was working with SAP HANA Predictive Analysis Library (SAP HANA 2.0 SPS05), I noticed an error in the procedure _SYS_AFL.PAL_LINEAR_REGRESSION_PREDICT. I’m wondering if any of you also got it.

According to the documentation columns of the parameter table are: „PARAM_NAME”, „INT_VALUE”, „DOUBLE_VALUE” and „STRING_VALUE”. Indeed, a table with such columns usually works and results are calculated correctly:

However, sometimes I get an error "Column ':PARAM_NAME' does not exist":

The same error is raised when the procedure is called with Python hana_ml library, but the names of columns that “do not exist” are different (without colons):

ERROR:hana_ml.algorithms.pal.linear_model:(256, 'sql processing error: "KDYMEK"."(DO statement)": line 13 col 1 (at pos 1233): search table error: _SYS_AFL.AFLPAL:FORECASTWITHLR_ANY: [102] (range 1) TypeMismatch exception: Column \'PARAM_NAME\' does not exist')
Traceback (most recent call last):
File "C:\Users\kdymek\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python310\lib\site-packages\hana_ml\algorithms\pal\linear_model.py", line 1069, in predict
self._call_pal_auto(conn,
File "C:\Users\kdymek\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python310\lib\site-packages\hana_ml\algorithms\pal\pal_base.py", line 859, in _call_pal_auto 
self.execute_statement = call_pal_auto_with_hint(conn_context,       
File "C:\Users\kdymek\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python310\lib\site-packages\hana_ml\algorithms\pal\pal_base.py", line 1365, in call_pal_auto_with_hint
 if try_exec(cur, sql, conn):
File "C:\Users\kdymek\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python310\lib\site-packages\hana_ml\algorithms\pal\pal_base.py", line 1323, in try_exec      
cur.execute(sql)
hdbcli.dbapi.Error: (256, 'sql processing error: "KDYMEK"."(DO statement)": line 13 col 1 (at pos 1233): search table error: _SYS_AFL.AFLPAL:FORECASTWITHLR_ANY: [102] (range 1) TypeMismatch exception: Column \'PARAM_NAME\' does not exist')

Workaround:

  1. renaming columns of the parameter table: ":PARAM_NAME, ":INT_VALUE", ":DOUBLE_VALUE", ":STRING_VALUE" (with colons) – doesn’t work in Python,
  2. or running the code once again as the error isn’t raised every time.

Do you know what causes this problem and how I can avoid it?

entry.title: FedML SAP Datasphere & Databricks
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am interested in applying FedML to Datasphere data in Databricks and subsequently deploying the inference results back into Datasphere, while also deploying the Machine Learning Model to SAP Kyma. Before proceeding, I have several technical questions that I would like clarified:

1) When I call an SAP Datasphere View for consumption in Databricks using SQL, does the data remain solely in SAP Datasphere, or is it also replicated into Databricks via the "select" command?

2) Is the configuration for the DBConnection between SAP Datasphere and Databricks the same as the connection setup between Databricks and SAP Kyma?

3) Can inference be performed on the deployed model within SAP Kyma?

4) Apart from Azure, Amazon, and Google, are there any other hyperscalers supported by Databricks that I might be overlooking?

I am looking forward to your responses. Thank you in advance.

Best regards from Germany.

entry.title: Re: Using PAL Procedures embeded in SQL Script (anonymous...
entry.content:
Hi Dirk,

based on your PAL procedure example, here is a simple example using anonymous blocks, you don't have to declare the structure of the PAL procedure output:

 

 

do
begin
tv_data = SELECT * FROM PAL_MULTIVARSTAT_DATA_TBL;
tv_parms = SELECT * FROM #PAL_PARAMETER_TBL;
CALL "_SYS_AFL"."PAL_MULTIVARIATE_ANALYSIS"(:tv_data, :tv_parms, tv_out);
SELECT * FROM :tv_out;
END;

 

 

The constraint if structures require to be declared or not comes from SQL Script.

Please note, usage of the Python Machine Learning client might be helpful, as you can script in Python and capture the ad-hoc generated SQL for the PAL procedure or even utilize methods to generate procedure code / even design-time artifacts. Just as another helping note.

I hope this is helpful, otherwise please share more details.

Best regards,

Christoph

entry.title: AI Core - Model Training and Inference for Multi output with multi Input types
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I’m working on a complex machine learning project using SAP AI Core, and I’ve hit a roadblock. The goal is to train and deploy a model that handles multiple input types (both numerical and categorical) and generates multiple outputs, which also include numerical and categorical values.

Problem Statement

 

  1. Model Training:
    • I have tried using models such as DecisionTreeRegressor, DecisionTreeClassifier, MultiOutputClassifier, and MultiOutputRegressor.
    • For encoding non-numerical fields, I’ve used LabelEncoder, OneHotEncoder, and MultiLabelBinarizer.
    • The training process sometimes fails, particularly when dealing with complex encoding schemes and multi-output formats.
    • Model Inference:
      • When training is successful, inference often fails. The primary issues are:
        • Difficulty in parsing multi-output results.
        • Challenges in converting outputs back to their original non-numerical forms.
        • Mapping values correctly to their respective output fields.

Detailed Steps and Issues

 

  1. Encoding:
    • For categorical inputs, I’ve tried both LabelEncoder for label encoding and OneHotEncoder for one-hot encoding.
    • For outputs, especially multi-label ones, I’ve used MultiLabelBinarizer.
    • However, combining these encoders with multi-output models seems to introduce complexity, particularly in maintaining the correct mappings during inference.
  2. Model Training:
    • Example code:

from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeRegressor

from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier

from sklearn.model_selection import KFold, train_test_split

from sklearn.inspection import permutation_importance

from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder

from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder

from sklearn.preprocessing import MultiLabelBinarizer

from sklearn.multioutput import MultiOutputClassifier

from sklearn.multioutput import MultiOutputRegressor

from sklearn.compose import make_column_transformer

# Assume X_train and y_train are preprocessed input and output datasets model =

clf = MultiOutputClassifier(DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=DT_MAX_DEPTH, random_state=31))  

    clf.fit(train_x, Y_encoded)

  • Issues:
  • Encoding-decoding mismatches.
  • Models not handling mixed input-output types effectively.

 

  1. Inference:
    • Example code:

query = dict(call_request.json)

    input_features = [ # list of values from request call

        query['Company_code'],

        query['Vendor'],

        query['Amount'],

        query['InvoiceDocumenttype'],

        query['Fiscalyear'],

    ]

 

# Convert to DataFrame to match the input structure of the encoder  

    input_df = pd.DataFrame([input_features], columns=['Company_code', 'Vendor', 'Amount', 'InvoiceDocumenttype', 'Fiscalyear'])  

 

    # Preprocess input_features using one-hot encoder  

    input_features_encoded = encoder_X.transform(input_df)

 

    # Make prediction  

    prediction = model.predict(input_features_encoded)  

 

    # Postprocess prediction to original labels  

    prediction = encoder_Y.inverse_transform(prediction)

    # Map the prediction to the field names

    fields = ['HKONT','KOSTL', 'Profitcenters', 'Paymentterms', 'Partnerbanktype', 'Taxcode']

    prediction_dict = dict(zip(fields, prediction[0]))

  • Issues:
    • Decoding predictions back to original categorical values.
    • Ensuring the predictions map correctly to their respective fields.

Questions

  • Has anyone successfully implemented a multi-input multi-output model that handles both numerical and categorical data?
  • Are there specific strategies or best practices for encoding and decoding in such scenarios?
  • Can you recommend any resources or examples that deal with similar complexities?

Any insights, suggestions, or references would be immensely helpful!

Thank you in advance for your assistance.
